summary: Linked and marked `melee/vi/vi0502.c` as matching. The PR restructures vi0502-local data into a `Vi0502Data` aggregate at `un_804000D0`, uses that aggregate for initial position and archive-name strings, adjusts local temporaries and argument extraction to match `vi0502_8031E124` and `un_8031E444_OnEnter`, and updates symbol metadata scopes/names for vi0502-related data. The automated report records five new matches including both touched functions and vi0502 data sections.

key_files.role: Main decomp source update for vi0502; introduces `Vi0502Data`, changes data/string access, adjusts locals and calls in matching functions.
evidence_refs: text_corpus:decomp-dev[bot] report new matches for main/melee/vi/vi0502

key_files.role: Symbol metadata adjusted for vi0502-related strings, sbss objects, and sdata2 floats, including local-scope labels and local scope for `erase_colors_vi0502` and `kirby_gobj`.

what_changed.text: `src/melee/vi/vi0502.c` replaces the standalone `Vec3 initial_pos` with a `Vi0502Data` struct containing `initial_pos` plus the strings `Vi0502.dat`, `visual0502Scene`, and `IrAls.dat`, backed by `un_804000D0`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:src/melee/vi/vi0502.c typedef Vi0502Data and final un_804000D0 initializer

what_changed.text: `vi0502_8031E124` now reads initial position through `un_804000D0.initial_pos`, sets player 1 facing direction to `-1.0f`, and uses a second `HSD_JObj*` local for the later `GET_JOBJ`/translation query.

`un_8031E444_OnEnter` now uses a local `Vi0502Data* data` for archive-load strings and extracts `p1_char_index`, `p1_costume_index`, and `p2_costume_index` into locals before calling `vi0502_8031E124`.

`symbols.txt` changes multiple vi0502-adjacent string and float symbols from global `un_...` names to local `@...` labels, and changes `erase_colors_vi0502` and `kirby_gobj` from global to local scope.
The automated report records new 100% matches for `vi0502_8031E124`, `un_8031E444_OnEnter`, and vi0502 `.data`, `.sbss`, and `.sdata2` items.

smart_moves.text: Grouped vi0502's initial vector and archive-name strings into a single data aggregate instead of keeping them as independent inline literals or a standalone vector, matching the reported `.data` completion for the unit.
Updated symbol metadata scopes alongside source changes, rather than compensating in C for local string, float, and sbss ownership.
Used small local-variable shape changes (`jobj2`, `data`, `costume1`, `costume2`) at the call sites that the report identifies as newly matching functions.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For matching work in vi scene files, nearby string literals and small constants may belong to a real TU data aggregate; verify section ownership and symbol metadata before deciding they should remain standalone literals or globals.

When a function is very close to matching, preserving or introducing seemingly redundant locals can be required by MWCC codegen at call boundaries; keep the tactic narrow and tie it to function-level match evidence.
Marking an object as `Matching` should be accompanied by narrow report evidence for the touched unit and any data sections affected by source or symbol metadata changes.
evidence_refs: text_corpus:decomp-dev[bot] five new matches for main/melee/vi/vi0502

naming_conventions.text: The PR uses a file-scoped semantic aggregate name `Vi0502Data` for recovered vi0502 data layout while retaining address-style global name `un_804000D0` for the backing symbol.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:src/melee/vi/vi0502.c typedef and extern/definition of un_804000D0

naming_conventions.text: Recovered struct fields are named directly after their observed roles or literal contents: `initial_pos`, `vi0502_dat`, `visual0502_scene`, and `irals_dat`.
Symbol metadata uses local `@NNN` labels for local strings/floats where the diff changes prior global `un_...` symbols.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Changed object status from nonmatching to matching only after source and symbol metadata changes for the same TU.
Introduced a data struct and accessed string/vector members through a local pointer or external aggregate to shape loads and data references.
Split repeated or direct expression uses into locals before a call, likely to match register/lifetime shape, but the PR does not include explicit objdiff narrative beyond the bot report.
Adjusted symbol scopes and local labels in `symbols.txt` for `.sdata`, `.sbss`, and `.sdata2` instead of adding dummy source storage.

review_feedback.text: decomp-dev[bot] reported matched code +0.03% (+1128 bytes), linked code +0.04% (+1468 bytes), matched data +0.01% (+112 bytes), linked data +0.01% (+128 bytes), with five new matches for `main/melee/vi/vi0502`.
No review comments or reviews are present in the supplied PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find other `src/melee/vi/*.c` files that use a scene-specific data aggregate containing initial positions and archive-name strings.
Search for PRs that change `Object(NonMatching, "melee/vi/*.c")` to `Object(Matching, ...)` together with `symbols.txt` local label updates.
Search for `lbArchive_LoadSymbols` calls in vi scene files that still use inline archive strings where section metadata may indicate a data aggregate.
Search for `un_804000D0` and adjacent `.data` ownership to verify whether `Vi0502Data` should be documented in path facts.
Search for vi scene functions using extra locals before final call dispatch as a matching tactic.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use real data aggregates when section evidence ties literals and vectors together
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2673 matched vi0502 by replacing a standalone `initial_pos` and inline archive strings with a `Vi0502Data` aggregate at `un_804000D0`, while also updating symbol metadata for local strings/floats. Curator should consider this as a vi-scene example of using section/data ownership rather than leaving unrelated standalone literals.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Narrow local-variable lifetime shaping can close near-matched vi functions
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2673 added narrow locals (`jobj2`, `data`, `costume1`, `costume2`) in functions reported as newly 100% matched. Keep this as a tactic candidate only when tied to narrow objdiff or bot-report evidence.

source_update_candidates.title: `src/melee/vi/vi0502.c` became matching in PR 2673
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2673 changed `configure.py` from `Object(NonMatching, "melee/vi/vi0502.c")` to `Object(Matching, "melee/vi/vi0502.c")`; the bot report lists `vi0502_8031E124`, `un_8031E444_OnEnter`, `.data`, `.sbss`, and `.sdata2` as new matches for `main/melee/vi/vi0502`.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential path fact about historical matching status and affected unit; curator should verify current source before adding.

source_update_candidates.title: vi0502 uses `Vi0502Data` at `un_804000D0` for initial position and archive strings
source_update_candidates.text: The PR introduces `Vi0502Data` with `initial_pos`, `vi0502_dat`, `visual0502_scene`, and `irals_dat`, defines `un_804000D0`, and updates vi0502 code to read those fields.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful file-specific data-layout fact, but should be checked against current source and symbol ownership before adding to path facts.

source_update_candidates.title: Symbol metadata can be the right fix for local data-section ownership
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2673 paired source matching with `symbols.txt` changes from global `un_...` names to local labels/scopes for vi0502 strings/floats/sbss, avoiding fake C storage. Existing standards already cover data ownership; curator may decide whether this PR should be indexed as an example rather than a new standard.
source_update_candidates.reason: This reinforces existing data-sections and literals/data-ownership standards; likely better as an example than a new rule.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all vi scene archive strings should be moved into structs; this PR only proves vi0502's observed data layout.
Do not treat added locals like `jobj2`, `costume1`, or `costume2` as general style; evidence only supports them as narrow matching choices in this PR.
Do not promote the PR's bot report into a full validation ledger with build/objdiff details; the slice contains only the automated summary.
Do not infer human reviewer intent or acceptance rationale; no human review comments are loaded.
